reviewTitle: Moisturizing & repairs skin barrier, but does pill.
reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on May 15, 2026
reviewText: Given myself a couple weeks of consistent use before giving a review. There are a lot of pros to this product which include: moisturizing and stays moisturized all day, made my PIE marks (post acne marks) fade a lot faster. Repairs my skin barrier, especially while using... Given myself a couple weeks of consistent use before giving a review. There are a lot of pros to this product which include: moisturizing and stays moisturized all day, made my PIE marks (post acne marks) fade a lot faster. Repairs my skin barrier, especially while using tretinoin nightly, not too thick but thick enough to keep my face hydrated. It does not have a smell. The cons are: slight burn for about 15 seconds but disappears, it pills if I apply too much. It does pill less if I pat it on my face versus rubbing; it does not pill when I apply liquid foundation. I just have to let it dry for 15-20 mins before applying makeup. I found the pros to overpower the cons and will continue to use this product.

reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on April 20, 2026
reviewText: I’ve been using the La Roche-Posay Toleriane Double Repair Face Moisturizer for a few weeks now, and it’s honestly one of the best daily moisturizers I’ve tried—especially for sensitive skin. First thing I noticed is how lightweight it feels. It absorbs quickly... I’ve been using the La Roche-Posay Toleriane Double Repair Face Moisturizer for a few weeks now, and it’s honestly one of the best daily moisturizers I’ve tried—especially for sensitive skin. First thing I noticed is how lightweight it feels. It absorbs quickly and doesn’t leave that greasy or heavy film that a lot of “hydrating” lotions do. My skin feels instantly softer, but not suffocated. It layers really well under makeup too—no pilling or weird texture. What really stands out is how balanced my skin has been since using it. I tend to get dry patches but also occasional breakouts, and this somehow helps with both. It hydrates without clogging pores, and I’ve noticed less redness overall. The fact that it contains ceramides and niacinamide is a huge plus if you’re trying to repair your skin barrier. It’s also fragrance-free, which I personally look for because anything scented tends to irritate my skin. The only small downside is the price point compared to drugstore moisturizers, but honestly, a little goes a long way and the quality makes it worth it. Overall, I’d definitely recommend this if you have sensitive, combination, or dry skin and want something simple but effective. It’s become a staple in my routine.

reviewText: I bought this moisturizer at a time when my skin truly needed help, with mature and sensitive skin, and I was dealing with intense dryness. My skin felt tight, dull, and uncomfortable, even after trying several products that didn’t provide lasting hydration. I... I bought this moisturizer at a time when my skin truly needed help, with mature and sensitive skin, and I was dealing with intense dryness. My skin felt tight, dull, and uncomfortable, even after trying several products that didn’t provide lasting hydration. I decided to try this cream and within the first few days, I noticed a real difference. It’s lightweight, absorbs quickly, doesn’t feel greasy, and most importantly, it doesn’t irritate my skin—instead, it soothes it. With continued use, my skin became softer, more supple, and visibly healthier, without that constant tight feeling. I love that it’s fragrance-free, oil-free, and non-comedogenic, as it hasn’t caused any breakouts. It also contains ceramides and niacinamide, and the added SPF makes my routine much easier. After trying many products, I can honestly say this is the only one that truly worked for me. It’s now a daily essential.

reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on May 25, 2026
reviewText: I have dry, sensitive skin and have been using this morning and night for a few weeks. What I like most is that it feels lightweight but still keeps my skin comfortable throughout the day. Some moisturizers for sensitive skin feel heavy or greasy, but this one absorbs quickly and layers well over serum without pilling. It’s fragrance fee, oil free and doesn’t irritate my skin or make me red, which is a huge plus. My skin feels softer and more balanced after using it consistently. I also like that it works well under makeup during the day. Pros: - Lightweight but hydrating - Good for sensitive skin - No strong scent - Absorbs quickly - Works well under makeup Cons: - May not be rich enough for super dry skin in winter - Price is a little high for the size Overall, I think it’s a solid everyday moisturizer if you want something gentle, simple and reliable without feeling heavy.

reviewText: I really tried to like this product but even after 6 weeks it is still aggravating my redness. You can see I applied this product to the left of my face (in the photo it appears on your right). I feel like I can see a marked redness from this product alone. I naturally have sensitive skin, redness, and acne. I have a history of being on Accutane so my skin can be fairly dry and flaky despite the humid conditions I live in. I use other LP products and love them, but this one slightly stings when applying and still aggravates my skin even after going through most of the product to give a fair review. At first I really thought maybe I just needed to give the product time, but I don’t think it’s a good fit for my skin. I need a product that is moisturizing without being too heavy as I am prone to clogged pores and I find I get greasy easy living in Hawaii. I thought the niacinamide might help my post acne dark spots and never reacted to this ingredient before. It doesn’t sting elsewhere on my body, so I’ll use the rest of the container elsewhere. Really hoping I can find a product that works for my skin…

reviewTitle: Great product, but packaging is not so great.
reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on March 9, 2026
reviewText: This is one of my favorite moisturizers, however, the dispensing tube leaves a lot to be desired. There is always so much left in the tube that won’t squeeze out when it starts to get low. I would say about 1/4 of the product stays at the top of the tube and won’t come out. If I didn’t cut the tube in half to reach the additional moisturizer that’s left in the tube, I would be getting a lot less product. I will attach pictures of the amount I wasn’t able to squeeze out of my last tube, but had to take it out manually in order to not waste any product. I would give this product five stars if it were not for the horrible packaging. LaRoche-Posay should find a better way to dispense their great product.

reviewTitle: A Staple in My Skincare Routine – Especially for Travel & Dry Skin!
reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on February 22, 2026
reviewText: This face cream has truly earned a permanent spot in my skincare routine. The texture is soft and velvety with absolutely no scent, which I really appreciate—especially since my skin can be sensitive. It dispenses easily, and the consistency is just right: not too greasy or heavy, but also not too light. It strikes that perfect balance and soaks in beautifully. One of my favorite things is how well it layers under makeup. It creates a smooth base without pilling or feeling slick, and it works just as well at night as it does during the day. I travel constantly—including ultra-long 23-hour flights from LAX to Hong Kong—and this cream always comes with me in my carry-on. Airplane cabins are incredibly drying, and applying this mid-flight makes a noticeable difference in keeping my skin hydrated and comfortable. I’ve also been in a cold climate for the past three months, and this has significantly helped with my dry skin. For reference, I’m over 50 with dry, menopausal skin, and my dermatologist actually recommended this for my rosacea. I’ve seen a substantial improvement in redness and overall skin comfort since using it consistently. It’s gentle, effective, and reliable. I will absolutely continue using this as a routine staple and highly recommend it—especially if you travel often, live in a dry climate, or struggle with sensitive or mature skin.

reviewTitle: Applies thick on skin and not sticky
reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on January 19, 2026
reviewText: I have sensitive skin, so I’m always cautious about trying new moisturizers. This one works very well for me and has not caused any breakouts. It applies smoothly and absorbs nicely without leaving a sticky or greasy feeling, which I really appreciate. I also have dry skin, and this moisturizer keeps my face hydrated throughout the day without looking flaky or feeling tight. It lasts all day without needing to reapply, and a small, pea sized amount is enough to cover my entire face. I’ve reordered this moisturizer multiple times, and it has become my go to for everyday use. I rated this product four stars, not because of the moisturizer itself, but due to the packaging. The box arrived crushed, likely during shipping. Fortunately, the product inside was undamaged, but the overall presentation was disappointing.

reviewText: I have very sensitive skin and finding a facial moisturizer that hydrates well without causing irritation can be a challenge. This one has been fantastic. It feels lightweight going on but still gives enough moisture that my skin stays soft and comfortable all day. No... I have very sensitive skin and finding a facial moisturizer that hydrates well without causing irritation can be a challenge. This one has been fantastic. It feels lightweight going on but still gives enough moisture that my skin stays soft and comfortable all day. No strong fragrance, no burning, no breakouts, just simple and effective skincare. I also really like that it layers well under makeup and doesn’t leave my face feeling greasy or sticky. The ceramides and hyaluronic acid make my skin feel healthier and more balanced overall. Vanicream continues to be one of the few brands my skin consistently tolerates well. I’ve already repurchased this and will keep using it.

reviewText: This moisturizer contains nothing fancy — and that’s the whole point. The ingredients are few but effective, with several ceramides and hyaluronic acid for deep and long-lasting hydration. Best of all, it contains no silicones or parabens. Because it’s noncomedogenic, it... This moisturizer contains nothing fancy — and that’s the whole point.  The ingredients are few but effective, with several ceramides and hyaluronic acid for deep and long-lasting hydration.  Best of all, it contains no silicones or parabens.  Because it’s noncomedogenic, it doesn’t cause breakouts from clogged pores. Since I have sensitive skin, all of the above are critical for my skin care routine. It’s about as simple and as effective as a cream can get. Because of that, it doesn’t contains any sunscreen, retinol, or other anti-wrinkle components.

reviewText: I’ve struggled with dry sensitive skin and eczema my entire life and this is now my ride or die facial moisturizer. I’ve struggled with dry sensitive skin and eczema my entire life and this is now my ride or die facial moisturizer. It’s so much less expensive than luxury brands that I have tried and works better. It absorbs so well, leaves no residue, and a small amount goes a long way. I will say, if you have an active eczema flare it can sting a tiiiiny bit but that’s an eczema thing. It causes zero irritation or inflammation there’s just some sensation as it goes on. Vanicream as a brand in general is soooo eczema friendly. Life changing.

reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on March 9, 2026
reviewText: So I don't typically leave reviews, but I feel like this lotion has been fantastic to me. I typically don't use lotion because I hate the feel, but my face really needed it. I have dry/oily skin. My skin gets gross and flaky, yet it's pretty oily. I've... So I don't typically leave reviews, but I feel like this lotion has been fantastic to me. I typically don't use lotion because I hate the feel, but my face really needed it. I have dry/oily skin. My skin gets gross and flaky, yet it's pretty oily. I've always dealt with it. I decided to go ahead and buy this stuff because I couldn't take my dry skin. It helps soooo much and you barely need any. I use a dime sized amount and really focus on my problem areas. It lasts all day, not too thick and not too watery. Not greasy at all for me and soaks in very nicely. Again, it lasts all day and I don't need touch ups, even on bad skin days. I don't ever break out and it's never clogged my pores, but to be fair I've never really had skin that's broken out much before. The one thing I did notice, before using this stuff I started noticing more fine lines around my eyes/forehead. I've been using this stuff for about a year and you can barely see them anymore. I have haven't changed my diet, it's pretty atrocious honestly, so I really feel this lotion has worked wonders on its own. Also I still have a decent amount left in the bottle and I bought it almost a year ago. I'm going to try their body lotion next. Hope this helps, or at least give it a whirl!

reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on May 13, 2026
reviewText: It was good until it literally started burning my face out of nowhere, that’s when I noticed they added Pentylene Glycol as an ingredient, which is known to irritate skin ( especially sensitive skin, which is what this product is catered towards). I could literally feel my face heating up as I applied this moisturizer and that’s just not something I want to deal with. Sorry Vanicream, this product was good while it lasted but I prefer not having my face on fire in the morning. And just for the record I do not have sensitive skin, so for those who do I’d be wary of this product.

However, for my combination skin (oily T-zone), it felt a bit too heavy and made my skin look more oily throughout the day. It didn’t absorb as quickly as I expected and left a slightly greasy finish.

reviewTitle: At this rate, I'm leaving this to my kids in my will
reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on May 18, 2026
reviewText: Ten months in and I have barely made a dent in this tub. I am genuinely starting to consider my options — mixing it into smoothies, using it as a cooking fat, applying it to every surface in the house. Something has to be done because at the current rate of consumption this... Ten months in and I have barely made a dent in this tub. I am genuinely starting to consider my options — mixing it into smoothies, using it as a cooking fat, applying it to every surface in the house. Something has to be done because at the current rate of consumption this container will outlive me. In all seriousness, the value proposition here is almost absurd. CeraVe Moisturizing Cream is a legitimately great product — dermatologist recommended, ceramides, hyaluronic acid, fragrance free, non-comedogenic, works on face and body, gentle enough for sensitive skin. It does everything a moisturizer should do without irritating your skin or smelling like you bathed in a candle. The 19oz tub is the right call if you actually plan to use moisturizer regularly. A little goes a long way, which is both the best and most frustrating thing about it. Best because you're getting incredible value per ounce. Frustrating because you will be looking at this same tub for the foreseeable future with no end in sight. Great product, generational quantity. Buy it once, pass it down. One star deducted because I now have to figure out what to do with the remaining 18.5 ounces.

reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on April 9, 2026
reviewText: I used to spend way too much on high end body moisturizers thinking the price meant better results. Tried CeraVe on a whim and realized I was paying for packaging and fragrance this whole time. This does more for my skin than anything I used before. I use it... I used to spend way too much on high end body moisturizers thinking the price meant better results. Tried CeraVe on a whim and realized I was paying for packaging and fragrance this whole time. This does more for my skin than anything I used before. I use it daily all over my body after showering and my skin stays soft the entire day. No irritation ever. The formula is so gentle that I forget I'm even wearing anything. No fragrance, no stinging, no breakouts. Just clean hydration. If you have sensitive skin and you're nervous about trying something new, this is about as safe as it gets. The 19 oz tub lasts a long time even with daily full body use. Ceramides and hyaluronic acid at this price point is honestly ridiculous value. Save your money on the luxury stuff and just get this.

reviewTitle: If you use tretinoin this is for you!
reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on April 1, 2026
reviewText: This moisturizer has been a total game changer for my skin. I’ve been using tretinoin, and my skin became extremely sensitive—dry, irritated, and compromised. After adding this cream into my routine, I noticed a huge difference. It helped restore my skin barrier,... This moisturizer has been a total game changer for my skin. I’ve been using tretinoin, and my skin became extremely sensitive—dry, irritated, and compromised. After adding this cream into my routine, I noticed a huge difference. It helped restore my skin barrier, and my skin feels calmer, more hydrated, and much less reactive. The ingredients are simple and gentle, which is exactly what sensitive skin needs—nothing unnecessary or irritating. I’ve tried much more expensive moisturizers, and honestly, I would pass on them and stick with this. It performs better for me without the high price tag. I also love how versatile it is—I use it on my face, neck, and body. It’s rich and nourishing without feeling greasy, and it absorbs beautifully. If you have sensitive skin or are using tretinoin, this is definitely worth it.

reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on March 6, 2026
reviewText: I have very dry skin, and around my chin and nose, it flakes and turns red, exposing my inner layer of skin due to severe dryness. I could never seem to hydrate it or get rid of the flakiness. I thought it was just my skin at this point, and I could do nothing to solve it.... I have very dry skin, and around my chin and nose, it flakes and turns red, exposing my inner layer of skin due to severe dryness. I could never seem to hydrate it or get rid of the flakiness. I thought it was just my skin at this point, and I could do nothing to solve it. Now, I've never considered using Cerave; I've always heard negative things about it. However, I've never been a makeup person until now, when I want to try to elevate my look (not to mention I'm always in pain from a lack of hydration). The problem is, my skin is so dry that it cracks the makeup, preventing it from setting well on my face. Another problem is that around my nostrils, the flakiness looks like boogers, nasty. It's incredibly annoying. So I did a little research, and now I'm here. LET ME TELL YOU! This cream is amazing! I love how thick it is, and how a little goes a long way! My face, for the first time in my short life, is not dry! It's not buttery smooth, as in your hand won't glide against my face, but it won't feel the flakiness and roughness of my skin—and that's a game changer! I'm genuinely obsessed with randomly rubbing my face to feel the almost barrier of hydration, especially around my nostrils! I cannot explain the level of amazement I felt when I touched my nostril area to feel practically smooth skin. I felt as if it were a miracle. I've only used it for a week now, keep in mind, and this is how I feel. PLEASE BUY THIS IF YOU'RE LIKE ME! I'M SO HAPPY WITH THIS PURCHASE, AND IT'S WORTH ALL MY MONEY 🙏

reviewTitle: It's not glamorous but it works wonders - even while I am on Accutane!
reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on February 5, 2026
reviewText: This is the best, most luxurious lotion I have ever used. And the only lotion that I can even stand to be on my skin. I started Accutane (for acne) last year and was told that my skin would dry out horribly and that I needed to find a good moisturizer to limit... This is the best, most luxurious lotion I have ever used.  And the only lotion that I can even stand to be on my skin. I started Accutane (for acne) last year and was told that my skin would dry out horribly and that I needed to find a good moisturizer to limit the peeling and cracking that I would inevitably experience.  Prior to the medicine my skin was oily and very acne prone and every lotion that I had tried either made me feel like I was wearing a latex suit or broke me out horribly.  After doing some research I read that this was a great solution and figured I would give it a try.  I started it out right away to try to avoid getting dry and was shocked at how well it worked.  Even at the beginning when I was still oily this lotion never felt too heavy.  Now don't get me wrong-it goes on like frosting and initially feels like a lot-but within minutes it soaks in and leaves your skin feeling like a little kids (not the sticky kind of little kid though-the good clean little kids with undamaged skin).  It will last for days too.  No need to reapply because it has soaked in to your skin and doesn't just sit on the surface.  My skin has never been this soft.  And here I am, nearly 9 months in to my Accutane medication regimen and I am not flaking or peeling.  In fact, my skin has never looked better or more healthy.  My doctors are shocked at how my skin has stayed moisturized because nearly everyone peels.  And it doesn't break you out which is obviously something super important.  Now, could I be an anomoly?  Maybe, but I don't think so.  I honestly think this lotion is Just. That. Good!  The price isn't bad either!

reviewTitle: La mejor crema hidratante para pieles sensibles.
reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on May 11, 2026
reviewText: He probado muchísimas cremas porque sufro de dermatitis seborreica y mi piel es extremadamente delicada. Muchas cremas para piel seca me causaban más brotes o me dejaban la cara brillante, pero con esta CeraVe azul la experiencia ha sido totalmente diferente. Lo que realmente siento Alivio inmediato, porque cuando tengo las zonas rojas o con picazón, me la pongo y siento que la piel se calma al momento. No arde para nada, que es lo que más me preocupaba. La textura es perfecta aunque se ve espesa en el tarro, cuando te la pones se absorbe súper rápido. Puedo maquillarme después o simplemente seguir con mi día sin sentir la cara pegajosa. Así que dile adiós a las "escamitas" que tanto molestan. Me ha ayudado muchísimo con la descamación que se me hacía alrededor de la zona T de mi cara y centro del pecho. Mantiene la piel hidratada de verdad por muchas horas. Es lo máximo y la recomendaría hoy mañana y siempre.

reviewText: I absolutely love the CeraVe Moisturizing Cream! This has become a staple in my daily skincare routine. It’s rich and deeply hydrating without feeling greasy or heavy. It absorbs easily and keeps my skin soft and smooth all day long — even in dry weather. I use it on both my face and body, and it’s gentle enough for sensitive skin. My skin feels healthier, more comfortable, and visibly more moisturized. The texture is perfect and the pump/jar packaging is convenient. Highly recommend this moisturizer to anyone looking for effective, everyday hydration. During winter it really protects my skin from dryness.

reviewText: Tenía la piel muy seca y probé varias cremas sin mucho resultado, hasta que encontré esta CeraVe. Desde las primeras aplicaciones noté que la piel se sentía más nutrida y menos tirante. reviewTitle: A Reliable Moisturizer with a Few Quirks
reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on January 23, 2026
reviewText: When I first opened the jar of Moisturizing Cream, I was struck by its rich, velvety texture. It felt substantial, almost like a luxurious treat for my skin. I was expecting something lighter, but this cream has a certain heft to it that suggests it means business. Applying it for the first time, I was curious to see if it would live up to its promise of quick absorption without leaving a greasy residue. One thing I noticed right away was how smoothly it glided over my skin. The cream absorbed faster than I anticipated, leaving my skin feeling soft and hydrated. It's become a staple in my daily routine, especially for those pesky dry patches that seem to appear out of nowhere. However, I did find myself wishing it had a bit more staying power throughout the day. By evening, my skin felt like it could use another application.

reviewText: Don’t be fooled by their photo. This is just a big tub that you need to stick your fingers in. Why would a product in a tub this size from a company that also sells a “refill“ not come with a pump dispenser? If I had kept the pump dispenser cap from the product I ran out of it wouldn’t be so annoying, but to me this is dumb. There is no pump dispenser supplied. There is just a cap on a jug. And, on top of that, their customer support website does not allow you to send a support request without accepting their privacy policies, and the checkbox on their privacy policies is not engageable, so I have found no way to send them a support request. Disappointing.

reviewTitle: This Is That ‘Boring’ Skincare That Quietly Works So Well
reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on May 25, 2026
reviewText: I’ve tried a lot of moisturizers over the years, and this falls into what people call “boring skincare” — and honestly, that’s exactly why it works so well. If you have acne-prone skin and deal with redness or irritation, this is one of those rare products that... I’ve tried a lot of moisturizers over the years, and this falls into what people call “boring skincare” — and honestly, that’s exactly why it works so well. If you have acne-prone skin and deal with redness or irritation, this is one of those rare products that checks both boxes without overcomplicating things. The consistency is lightweight but still hydrating. It spreads easily and absorbs quickly into the skin without leaving any greasy or heavy residue. It gives my skin the hydration it needs without clogging pores or triggering breakouts. I’ve had no issues with acne while using this, which is not something I can say for most moisturizers. It also has Very minimal to no fragrance, which I prefer — especially great for sensitive or reactive skin. Overall: This isn’t a “flashy” product, but it’s consistent, gentle, and effective. It’s the kind of moisturizer you can rely on daily without worrying about irritation or breakouts.

reviewTitle: Highly recommended for all skin types!
reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on April 9, 2026
reviewText: Game-Changer Night Moisturizer – Lightweight, Hydrating & Perfect for Sensitive Skin I’ve been using the CeraVe PM Facial Moisturizing Lotion for a few weeks now, and honestly, it has become a staple in my skincare routine. First thing I noticed is... Game-Changer Night Moisturizer – Lightweight, Hydrating & Perfect for Sensitive Skin I’ve been using the CeraVe PM Facial Moisturizing Lotion for a few weeks now, and honestly, it has become a staple in my skincare routine. First thing I noticed is how lightweight and fast-absorbing it is. It sinks into the skin within seconds without leaving any greasy or sticky feeling, which is perfect if you don’t like heavy night creams. Despite being lightweight, it provides deep hydration throughout the night—I wake up with soft, smooth, and refreshed skin. What really makes this product stand out is its ingredient combination: • Hyaluronic acid keeps my skin hydrated by locking in moisture • Niacinamide helps calm my skin and reduces redness • Ceramides (1, 3, 6-II) strengthen the skin barrier I also love that it’s fragrance-free, oil-free, and non-comedogenic, so it doesn’t clog pores or irritate my skin. I have slightly sensitive and acne-prone skin, and this has worked perfectly without causing breakouts.

reviewTitle: Lightweight, Gentle & perfect for sensitive skin, instant hydration
reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on April 4, 2026
reviewText: I received this CeraVe PM Facial Moisturizing Lotion this morning and tried it for the first time tonight, and I’m already really impressed. The texture is lightweight and smooth. It applies easily and absorbs quickly without leaving any greasy or sticky feeling.... I received this CeraVe PM Facial Moisturizing Lotion this morning and tried it for the first time tonight, and I’m already really impressed. The texture is lightweight and smooth. It applies easily and absorbs quickly without leaving any greasy or sticky feeling. My skin felt instantly hydrated, soft, and comfortable. What I like most is how gentle it is. I’m currently using treatments that make my skin more sensitive, and this moisturizer didn’t cause any irritation at all. It actually helped calm my skin and made it feel more balanced. I also appreciate that it contains ingredients like niacinamide, ceramides, and hyaluronic acid, which are great for supporting the skin barrier and hydration. After just one use, my skin already looks more supple and healthy. It’s a great option for nighttime, especially if you don’t like heavy creams. So far, I’m very happy with it and will definitely keep using it.

reviewTitle: Hidratante ligera perfecta para la noche
reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on April 1, 2026
reviewText: He estado usando esta loción por las noches y me ha gustado mucho. Es súper ligera, se absorbe rápido y no deja la piel grasosa, lo cual es ideal si tienes piel mixta o grasa como yo. Siento que hidrata bien sin tapar los poros ni causar brotes, y al día siguiente la piel se ve más suave y equilibrada. También la he usado junto con tratamientos para el acné y funciona bien porque no irrita. No tiene fragancia, lo cual es un plus si tienes piel sensible. La única cosa es que si tienes la piel muy seca, puede que necesites algo más pesado. En general, es una excelente opción para uso diario y definitivamente la volvería a comprar.

reviewTitle: Can use day and night with no lotion feeling! Was itchy first few days but went away.
reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on March 28, 2026
reviewText: As part of my skincare routine I bought this and a few other CeraVe products (cleanser, acne control gel, AM moisturizer). Since I have acne prone sensitive skin I was careful with introducing this to my routine (started with every other day then every day. If I don't go outside I use it twice a day in place of the AM moisturizer.) When I first started using this and the acne control gel my face itched but after about a week that went away. Unlike the AM moisturizer this doesn't have sunscreen in it so you can use this day and night, it's also a different consistency than the AM moisturizer. It's lightweight, doesn't leave any visible residue, and once it's dried and absorbs into your skin it doesn't even feel like you have anything on! No lotion feeling or anything which I love (I usually avoid lotions because I can feel them on my skin even after I rub them in and my skin absorbs them.) So I can just carry one with my day. Fantastic moisturizer 5/5 (was a 4/5 but updated to 5/5 as the itchiness goes away)

reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on March 1, 2026
reviewText: I’ve been using the CeraVe PM Facial Moisturizing Lotion as part of my nighttime routine and it has worked really well for my combination skin. The texture is lightweight, smooth, and absorbs quickly without leaving any greasy or heavy feeling. What I love most is how hydrating yet gentle it is. My skin feels soft, balanced, and calm by the morning. It layers nicely with my other skincare products and hasn’t caused any breakouts or irritation. The formula feels simple and reliable, which is exactly what I look for in a daily moisturizer. Overall, a great affordable staple for maintaining healthy, hydrated skin.

reviewTitle: Excellent Formula, but the Product Runs Out Far Too Quickly
reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on May 24, 2026
reviewText: The balm is genuinely hydrating, smooth, and very convenient to use. It applies beautifully, gives the skin a healthy glow, and works well on dry areas, lips, and the face overall. The texture feels comfortable and nourishing without feeling overly heavy or greasy.... The balm is genuinely hydrating, smooth, and very convenient to use. It applies beautifully, gives the skin a healthy glow, and works well on dry areas, lips, and the face overall. The texture feels comfortable and nourishing without feeling overly heavy or greasy. My only major disappointment is the value for the price. Even using a very thin layer across the face once each evening, one stick lasted only about a week. I purchased two of them, and both were exactly the same, so this was clearly not an issue with a defective or partially filled tube. If you intend to use it regularly over the full face, expect it to run out extremely quickly. I purchased mine during a significant sale, and even at the discounted price I still felt disappointed by how little use I got from it. Overall, the formula performs well, but the quantity does not justify the regular price for consistent daily use.

reviewText: An okay moisturizer. Very shiny. Scent is old fashioned - kind of a floral baby powder scent. Takes a while to soak in which is by design since it only fills in the wrinkles instead of plumping them. The illusion is in the shine. You can see from my photos that in certain... An okay moisturizer. Very shiny. Scent is old fashioned - kind of a floral baby powder scent. Takes a while to soak in which is by design since it only fills in the wrinkles instead of plumping them. The illusion is in the shine. You can see from my photos that in certain light the wrinkles appear slightly diminished due to the shine but you can clearly see they are still there in the shadows. While this example is on my hand, I had the same results under my eyes and on my forehead. I let the product soak in for 5 minutes in these photos. On the upside it worked temporary wonders on my KP!

reviewTitle: I don’t often leave reviews, but this exceeded my expectations and I LOVE it!!! I NEEDED it!
reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on May 5, 2026
reviewText: I don’t review things unless they REALLY “wow” me, rare, lol. My eyes had been watering for about a month from allergies, then last week my eyeballs were literally hurting so badly, which that had never happened to me before. So I got eye drops made with ketotifen (from... I don’t review things unless they REALLY “wow” me,  rare, lol.  My eyes had been watering for about a month from allergies, then last week my eyeballs were literally hurting so badly, which that had never happened to me before. So I got eye drops made with ketotifen (from the Walmart App) and it got rid of the pain in my eyeballs and stopped symptoms immediately, thank goodness. Then, from the constant watering, rubbing, dabbing and wiping my eyes, alll above, below and around my eyes became raw and chapped, burning and painful. I tried everything! Aquaphore, Goldbond, Pili Ani, and a lot of other brand name high quality face washes, moisturizers, masks, creams, oils and nothing was helping and some seemed to make it even worse. I saw this product and after reading the reviews, decided to give this one last shot before going to a Dermatologist. The moment I applied it, I felt INSTANT relief and soothing. I also was surprised to literally feel it tighten the skin! This morning I woke up to use it, day 3, and before I did I noticed my skin was so much better!!!…No longer painful, discolored or swollen & puffy, the skin was still silky soft from applying it last night, and my orbital skin is 97% better!!! The pink/red/purple has gone away, it’s no longer tender/burns to the touch, and you can literally feel it nourishing and tightening your skin. After begging, my husband let me try it on his skin (lol) just to make sure I wasn’t going koo koo. He noticed too! And commented about how soft it is! Yes~Exactly! It makes your skin like SILK! It is a tad oily when you first put it on, but it “settles” quickly into a gentle softness and doubles as a primer before you apply your makeup! It smooths out your pores and makes your skin so smooth.  Just this morning was the first time I applied makeup with it and was pleasantly surprised. I have tested it putting it on the top of one hand and nothing on the other, timed an hour and WOWWWWW the difference in undeniable! I personally recommend using it after washing your face before you put anything else on (moisturizer, makeup, etc). As long as you give it 10-15 minutes to let it absorb and settle, you’re good to go with or without makeup! I’m just really glad I found this because that skin is so sensitive and NEEDS this type of care. This product gets a solid 5 stars from me and I’m excited to make it part of my morning and evening routines! : )

reviewText: Me encantó este bálsamo de KAHI. Es súper fácil de usar porque viene en formato tipo barra, lo que lo hace perfecto para llevar en la cartera y aplicar en cualquier momento del día. Lo he estado usando en el rostro, especialmente en áreas secas como alrededor de los labios y debajo de los ojos, y realmente ayuda a mantener la piel hidratada y con un aspecto más suave. También lo probé en el cuello y funciona muy bien. No es pegajoso y deja un brillo natural bonito. Además, tiene un aroma suave que no molesta. Lo único a tener en cuenta es que si tienes la piel muy grasa, puede sentirse un poco pesado, pero para piel seca o normal es excelente. Definitivamente lo volvería a comptar

reviewText: KAHI Wrinkle Bounce Multi Balm Stick 53 year old female. Combination skin. Not breakout prone. I had a vitamin C stick that I liked but saw advertisements for this and thought I would give it a try. I really enjoy korean skincare lines and hoped this would follow suit. This was not the product for me. The texture was similar to vaseline. I can apply concealer over it but I feel it still is greasy. When used anywhere but under my eyes I would get a pimple bevause I am assuming it would clog my pores. Under my eyes, yes, it would keep them moisturized just like vaseline would. I still have it and will use it under my eyes when working from home so I don't waste it but I will not be buying it again.

reviewText: This balm is AMAZING! I can't believe what it's done for my crows feet and 11's. The hydration is absolutely awesome, I've never seen a skin product ACTUALLY WORK like this and give a noticeable change, especiallyso fast. I do still have crows feet when I laugh and smile but NOT with just resting face. Same with my 11's, they're still there just not as deep or noticeable until I frown or lower my brows. Consistency is kind of thick and greasy but I only applied at night, every night. This stuff really does work and although I gave 5 stars I do have 2 issues, the size and longevity 😕. Before I ordered this I was using a different balm, exact same size but it lasted almost 3 weeks LONGER. I started using the Kahi balm on January 5th and the tube was completely empty on January 30th. So since both balms work I already ordered the other brand since it lasts longer, but when that runs out I'm definitely ordering this again. I just got really upset over how fast it disappeared. It REALLY IS AN AMAZING PRODUCT THAT DOES WHAT IT SAYS, I just don't want to pay this price every 3 weeks. I will do every other month though haha I'm adding pics of the empty tube after only 3 weeks. It's the same size as my other balm, just didn't last as long. I used the emoji sticker things to cover the brand of the other balm, just showing they're the exact same size but the Kahi only last me like half the time. Regardless of my aggravation over it not lasting as long as I'd like, I can't deny how great this stuff is, it really is worth the hype. Highly recommend 🌟🌟🌟🌟🌟

reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on January 17, 2026
reviewText: I was really excited to try the KAHI Multi Balm, mainly because I was hoping to use it as a lip balm. Before using it, I read the instructions on the box and had to use Google Translate for some of the wording (I’ll be including photos of what it says). What immediately gave me pause was that the box specifically says to avoid contact with the eyes and mouth. That raised a big concern for me. If a product says to avoid contact with your mouth, then I personally don’t feel comfortable using it directly on my lips. After doing some digging, it seems this balm is actually meant to be used around the mouth, such as the corners of the lips, rather than directly on the lips themselves. I know many people use it as a lip balm anyway, but for me, that felt like a risk I wasn’t willing to take—because realistically, anything on your lips will end up in your mouth. As for the scent, I really didn’t like it. It smells very much like beeswax or bitter makeup—not sweet, not pleasant, just kind of waxy and off-putting. If you’re sensitive to smells, this is something to consider. Texture-wise, this product is extremely greasy. I mean very greasy. It doesn’t sink in quickly and leaves a heavy, slick feeling on the skin. If you use too much, it gets everywhere—hands, hair, pillowcases. If your hair is down, it will stick to your face. It just feels messy, sticky, and weighed down rather than soft or refreshed. This is definitely something you’d want to use very sparingly, and probably only at night. You can use this on other areas of the face, just be careful not to put it too close to the eyes, as that’s likely why some people experience irritation. That said, even on the face, I personally didn’t enjoy how it felt. I prefer going to bed with my skin feeling clean and comfortable—not oily and heavy. For the price ($22), this just wasn’t worth it for me. I won’t be reaching for it often, if at all. While it would probably last a long time if used sparingly, I don’t like the texture, the scent, or how messy it can get. I’m glad I was able to try it and form my own opinion, but this is not a product I would repurchase. If you’re curious, definitely read the box carefully and decide whether it fits your comfort level and skincare preferences. For me, it’s a two-star product.

reviewTitle: Convenient hydration and glow in one stick
reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on January 14, 2026
reviewText: I’ve been using the KAHI Wrinkle Bounce Multi Balm Stick for a few weeks, and it’s quickly become a staple in my skincare routine. The compact stick design makes it incredibly easy to carry around — I can throw it in my bag and use it anytime my skin feels dry or dull. The balm glides on smoothly and provides an instant boost of hydration. I especially like using it around my eyes, lips, and neck, where fine lines tend to show first. It leaves a subtle glow without feeling greasy, which makes it perfect as a quick touch-up over makeup. The formula claims up to 48 hours of hydration, and while I wouldn’t say it lasts that long for me, it definitely keeps my skin feeling soft and refreshed throughout the day. I also appreciate that it’s enriched with Jeju fermented oils and collagen, which give it a nourishing feel. After consistent use, I’ve noticed my skin looking a bit plumper and more radiant, especially around the nasolabial folds.

reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on May 23, 2026
reviewText: I’ll admit, I was skeptical about this “Botox on a stick” plumping product from the start. It’s been advertised everywhere with claims that it works instantly and gives you that smoother, fuller, younger look right away. After using it four times now, I honestly don’t see the hype. Instead of looking plumper or more youthful, my skin just feels greasy. It gives more of a shiny/oily finish than any real smoothing effect. Maybe that glow works for some people, but on me it honestly just looks like I put on too much product — kind of like a tan can make you look healthier, but shine alone doesn’t equal younger-looking skin. So far, I haven’t noticed any firming, tightening, or noticeable plumping. For me, it’s mostly hype and grease.

reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on May 1, 2026
reviewText: Call me easily influenced, because I fell hook, line, and sinker for the KAHI Wrinkle Bounce Multi Balm Stick with salmon PDRN and collagen. This lightly scented multi balm sits on top of the skin like petroleum jelly and does not sink in, which did very little for my oily skin and left me looking like a greasy mess. If anything, the longevity of the shiny slick served to dry out my delicate under eye area which accented my fine lines even more. Even when used under makeup, the results were less than impressive and cakey. The product is a pretty good size and having a twist-up tube is very convenient. Despite not working for my face, I was still able to use this multi balm on the backs of my hands, elbows, knees, and cuticles. I was determined to use the whole thing and even with regular use, this stick lasted a full year so I feel I got my money's worth. This was fairly priced and a little goes a long way. Maybe for drier skin girlies, the KAHI Wrinkle Bounce Multi Balm Stick is a must but for me, it is a nope.

reviewText: I've NEVER written an amazon review and here I am to warn you about this product so please hear me out! At first I thought the oil was nice and moisturizing, but a few days after using it I got a weird rash on my lip line and I've never had a rash like that before and hardly do I ever get allergic reactions to beauty products. A week into using it I started breaking out. I thought it was the weather or hormones so I tried using the product on my forehead wrinkle ONLY so I'd know if it's causing the breakouts. Sure enough, a couple days later I did get 1 big pimple and a few smaller bumps around it, where my forehead was clear and smooth before 😭. I want my old skin back!

reviewText: I really wanted to like this product, but the texture is extremely heavy and greasy, it feels more like petroleum jelly than a skincare balm. It sits on top of the skin instead of absorbing, and it leaves a shiny, oily layer that’s uncomfortable to wear during the day. After consistent use, I haven’t noticed any of the results that are so widely advertised. No visible improvement, no smoothing effect, nothing that justifies the price or the hype. Overall, this was a disappointing purchase. I wouldn’t buy it again.

reviewTitle: Goodbye dry, dull skin ✨ long-lasting hydration
reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on April 16, 2026
reviewText: I have very dry skin that can sometimes look flaky and dull, so I’m always trying new products to keep it hydrated. This balm ended up being a really nice surprise. The biggest thing I noticed is how quickly it works. As soon as I apply it, my skin looks more hydrated and has a soft, natural glow. It’s perfect for those moments when your skin looks tired or dry and you need a quick refresh. I use it in the morning after sunscreen, and it keeps my skin feeling comfortable and moisturized for hours. It doesn’t feel heavy as long as you don’t apply too much—just a small amount is enough. I also love how easy it is to carry and apply without using your hands. It’s great for quick touch-ups during the day, even over makeup. The only downside for me is the size. It’s a bit small, so I can see it running out pretty quickly with daily use. Overall, if you have dry skin and want something convenient that gives instant hydration and a natural glow, this is definitely worth trying.

reviewText: I really Love this product. I am not a skincare guru, yet I noticed and other people noticed a difference and youthful appearance in my skin.
